milestones.title: Implicit brownfield milestone
milestones.description: Auto-created by `pragma migrate` to wrap pre-v0.2 flat requirements. Safe to rename when the project grows real milestones.

slices.title: Implicit brownfield slice
slices.description: Auto-created by `pragma migrate`. All pre-v0.2 requirements live here until the user carves real slices.

milestones.title: Gate and slice
milestones.description: v0.2 gate state machine, slice lifecycle, unlock.

slices.title: Slice gate
slices.description: activate, unlock with red tests, complete.

slices.title: v0.3 hooks and battery
slices.description: Claude Code hooks + safety battery + integrity seal.

slices.title: v0.4 SDK, PIL, narrative
slices.description: Runtime SDK package, Post-Implementation Log, narrative artifacts.

slices.title: v1.0 greenfield bootstrap and determinism
slices.description: Greenfield init flow, plan-greenfield Pattern C, real doctor recovery, determinism acceptance.

slices.title: v1.0.1 patch - ruff rev alignment, freeze error serialisation, collector parser
slices.description: Stabilisation slice patching three bugs surfaced during the v1.0 release - ruff pre-commit rev skew vs local ruff, freeze crashing with non-serializable ValueError when a requirement references a non-existent milestone, and the pytest 9 collect-only parser returning zero nodeids when parent addopts pin -q.

slices.title: v1.0.5 span retention
slices.description: doctor --clean-spans with keep_runs / keep_days retention and manifest config. Closes the v1.0.4-parked paper cut that .pragma/spans/ accumulates forever.

slices.title: v1.0.6 bug sweep
slices.description: Closes BUG-015 (run_tests inherited -q), BUG-016 (slice cancel leaves stale manifest_hash), BUG-017 (greenfield scaffold missing src import glue), BUG-018 (ex-KI-13, slice complete subprocess cwd on nested tests_root), BUG-019 (ex-KI-14, deptry false-positives on pragma pyproject).

slices.title: v1.1.0 first-run PIL reachable
slices.description: Close BUG-020 (Pragma's internal pytest runs clear --junit-xml via -o addopts=, so PIL shows 0 verified on a fresh first-run project even when user follows the docs). Scope is the full proof chain - subprocess emits junit, report surfaces artifact gaps instead of silently returning "missing" everywhere, smoke script asserts the end-to-end PIL story, docs describe the loop as it is.

requirements.title: CLI emits stable JSON on success and failure
requirements.description: Every pragma subcommand exits zero with {ok:true,...} JSON on success and non-zero with {error:'code',message,remediation,context} JSON on failure, so Claude Code can branch on either path.

permutations.description: init emits {ok:true,created:[...]}
init on existing project emits already_initialised
freeze writes lock with sha256 hash
freeze without manifest emits manifest_not_found
verify manifest passes on fresh freeze
verify fails on stale lock with hash_mismatch
doctor always exits zero

requirements.title: Manifest and lockfile round-trip deterministically
requirements.description: The sha256 hash of pragma.yaml is stable across loads and changes only when the canonical YAML content changes. Lockfile writes are atomic (tempfile + rename) so an interrupted freeze never leaves a half-written lock.

permutations.description: hash_manifest is stable across two loads of the same yaml
hash_manifest changes when yaml content changes
write_lock leaves no .tmp files on success
read_lock on invalid json raises manifest_schema_error

requirements.title: Slice gate enforces test-first discipline
requirements.description:
Once a slice is activated the gate is LOCKED. `pragma unlock` flips
the gate only when every permutation of every requirement in the
active slice has a matching failing test per the naming convention
`test_req_<req_id>_<permutation_id>`. `pragma slice complete`
requires all slice tests to be green. `audit.jsonl` records every
transition.

permutations.description: slice activate transitions inactive -> LOCKED and writes audit
unlock fails when required tests are absent or passing
unlock succeeds when every permutation has a red test
slice complete fails if any required test is red
slice complete transitions UNLOCKED -> shipped when tests pass
every transition appends one line to audit.jsonl

requirements.title: Claude Code hooks and pre-commit battery enforce v0.3 contract
requirements.description:
SessionStart emits additionalContext with gate state (≤9500 chars).
PreToolUse denies src/ edits when LOCKED and denies non-touches
edits when UNLOCKED. PostToolUse blocks on discipline violations.
Stop runs `pragma verify all` and blocks on failure. `pragma hooks
seal/verify/show` manage the integrity hash. `pragma verify
discipline/commits/integrity` each have one exit code and one
remediation. `pragma init --brownfield` writes .claude/settings.json
+ hash + expanded .pre-commit-config.yaml.

permutations.description: SessionStart returns additionalContext with gate state
PreToolUse denies src/ edit when gate LOCKED
PreToolUse allows edits outside source_root
PostToolUse blocks on cyclomatic complexity > 10
Stop blocks turn-end when verify all fails
pragma hooks seal writes .pragma/claude-settings.hash
pragma verify integrity exits 1 on hash mismatch
pragma init --brownfield writes claude settings and hash

requirements.title: Runtime SDK and PIL dogfood
requirements.description:
Pragma's own tests emit @pragma.trace spans and pragma report
surfaces them as verified permutations in the PIL.

permutations.description: @trace decorator emits OTel span with pragma.logic_id
set_permutation attaches and detaches baggage cleanly
pytest plugin writes .pragma/spans/*.jsonl on session end
Test passes without matching span -> status mocked
pragma report --json output is byte-identical across runs
narrative commit output passes verify commits shape check
narrative pr includes Summary / REQs / PIL / review pointer

requirements.title: pragma report is byte-identical on repeat; manifest hash stable on noop freeze
requirements.description: Pragma's deterministic contract: two consecutive pragma report --json invocations must produce byte-identical output on the same commit, and pragma freeze on an unchanged pragma.yaml must not change its manifest hash. Locks in v1.0 done criterion #5 (§7.8) as an enforced test.

permutations.description: Two back-to-back pragma report --json invocations produce identical bytes
pragma freeze on an unchanged pragma.yaml leaves manifest_hash unchanged
pragma freeze on an unchanged pragma.yaml is a complete noop - pragma.lock.json bytes identical before and after

requirements.title: pre-commit ruff rev matches local ruff so format checks are stable
requirements.description: The .pre-commit-config.yaml ruff-pre-commit rev must match the ruff version pinned in packages/pragma/pyproject.toml, so ruff-format run locally and by pre-commit agree on output and CI does not need SKIP=ruff-format as a workaround. Patches the v1.0 release-time ruff-skew incident (v0.8.6 in pre-commit vs 0.15.11 locally).

permutations.description: pre-commit run ruff-format --all-files is a noop on a tree already clean under local ruff
CI format check passes without SKIP=ruff-format

requirements.title: freeze emits structured JSON error when a requirement has null milestone or slice
requirements.description: When pragma.yaml contains a requirement without milestone or slice assignment, pragma freeze must exit non-zero with a JSON payload {error:"requirement_unassigned", message, remediation, context:{requirement_id, missing_fields}} instead of crashing with a Python traceback. Fixes the v1.0 release-time bug where ValueError was non-serializable through the JSON error path.

permutations.description: freeze on a manifest where a requirement has no milestone exits 1 with requirement_unassigned JSON
freeze on a manifest where a requirement has milestone but no slice exits 1 with requirement_unassigned JSON
freeze on a manifest where every requirement has milestone and slice assigned succeeds

requirements.title: collect_tests finds pytest 9 nodeids regardless of inherited addopts
requirements.description: pragma.core.tests_discovery.collect_tests must return per-test nodeids even when the parent pyproject pins -q (or other verbosity-suppressing flags) in addopts. Before v1.0.1, inherited `-q` under pytest 9 compacted collect-only output into file-summary form (`file.py: 4`) so the nodeid parser returned zero hits and every slice unlock reported all required tests as missing.

permutations.description: collect_tests finds test functions when a parent pyproject sets addopts = "-q"
collect_tests without parent addopts still works

requirements.title: doctor --clean-spans prunes old span files by retention policy
requirements.description: `pragma doctor --clean-spans` removes old `.pragma/spans/*.jsonl` files to bound disk growth over long-running projects. Retention is configured via `spans_retention:` in `pragma.yaml` (keys `keep_runs` and `keep_days`, both optional; default `keep_runs: 50` when the block is absent) or overridden at the CLI with `--keep-runs N` / `--keep-days N`. `--dry-run` reports what would be deleted without touching the filesystem. Non-dry runs append a `spans_cleaned` event to `audit.jsonl` with files_removed, bytes_freed, and the strategy used. `doctor` base mode (no flag) reports `spans_count` and `spans_bytes` in its diagnostics so users see accumulation before it hurts.

permutations.description: with N+K files present, --keep-runs N removes the K oldest span files
--keep-days D preserves files with mtime within D days and removes older ones
--clean-spans --dry-run reports deletions without removing any file
--clean-spans with no flags and no manifest config defaults to keep_runs=50
--clean-spans with no CLI flags honours pragma.yaml spans_retention block
non-dry --clean-spans appends a spans_cleaned event to audit.jsonl
plain `pragma doctor` includes spans_count and spans_bytes in diagnostics

requirements.title: run_tests overrides inherited addopts
requirements.description: `pragma.core.tests_discovery.run_tests` must clear inherited pytest `addopts` via `-o addopts=` so that a user `pytest.ini` or `pyproject.toml` pinning `-q` does not compress pytest output below what run_tests regex can parse. Before v1.0.6, a greenfield `pytest.ini` with `addopts = -q` made `pragma slice complete` report every test as `error` even when the tests passed, because `-q` turned per-test `PASSED/FAILED` lines into `.`/`F` dots. Matches the fix already in `collect_tests` (v1.0.3, BUG-009/KI-12).

permutations.description: run_tests reports the correct verdicts when a parent pytest.ini pins -q in addopts
run_tests without parent addopts still returns correct verdicts

requirements.title: slice cancel resets state to fully neutral
requirements.description: `pragma slice cancel` must reset `.pragma/state.json` to the default neutral shape bound to the current lock hash, not just null out `active_slice`. Before v1.0.6, cancel left the previous `manifest_hash` in state, so `pragma verify gate` failed with `gate_hash_drift` if the manifest had changed since activation, and `doctor --emergency-unlock` refused because it saw state as already neutral. The user had no in-tool recovery path and had to delete `.pragma/state.json` by hand.

permutations.description: after cancel, state.manifest_hash equals the current lock hash
pragma verify gate is green after cancel even if manifest was rewritten mid-slice

requirements.title: greenfield scaffold ships src import glue
requirements.description: `pragma init --greenfield --language python` must scaffold tests in a way that allows `from <module> import ...` where `<module>` lives in `src/`. Before v1.0.6 the scaffold wrote `src/` and `tests/` but no `conftest.py` or `pyproject.toml` wiring, so the first test file written per the documented convention raised ImportError and blocked `pragma unlock`. Fix: the init template writes `tests/conftest.py` that inserts `<project>/src` into `sys.path`.

permutations.description: pragma init --greenfield writes tests/conftest.py
a fresh greenfield tree can collect a test that imports from src/

requirements.title: slice complete subprocess cwd honours manifest source layout
requirements.description: BUG-018 (ex-KI-13). `pragma.core.tests_discovery.run_tests` and `collect_tests` invoke pytest with `cwd=tests_dir.parent`, which breaks on brownfield workspaces where `tests_root` is more than one directory deep (e.g. Pragma's own `packages/pragma/tests/`): the emitted nodeids are relative to the user cwd but pytest is chdir'd below that, so it looks for them at a doubled path and every test reports as `error`. Fix: accept a `cwd` parameter defaulting to `Path.cwd()` from the caller, and thread the real project root through.

permutations.description: collect_tests returns nodeids that run_tests can consume when tests_root is packages/foo/tests/
run_tests returns the correct verdicts when tests_root is packages/foo/tests/

requirements.title: pragma package pyproject separates runtime and dev deps
requirements.description: BUG-019 (ex-KI-14). `packages/pragma/pyproject.toml` lists ruff, mypy, pytest-cov, pre-commit, types-PyYAML, opentelemetry-api, opentelemetry-sdk under `[project.dependencies]` — but they are not imported by pragma at runtime. Deptry correctly reports them as DEP002 unused dependencies. Fix: move them to `[project.optional-dependencies.dev]` so `pip install pragma` pulls only real runtime deps and `pip install pragma[dev]` pulls the full set. After fix, deptry hook runs clean on both packages/pragma and packages/pragma-sdk.

permutations.description: packages/pragma/pyproject.toml project.dependencies contains none of ruff/mypy/pre-commit/pytest-cov/types-PyYAML/opentelemetry-api/opentelemetry-sdk
packages/pragma/pyproject.toml project.optional-dependencies.dev contains all the dev tools
deptry src exits 0 with no issues on packages/pragma

requirements.title: pragma's internal pytest runs emit junit.xml by default
requirements.description: BUG-020. `pragma.core.tests_discovery.run_tests` passes `-o addopts=` to clear inherited `-q` from user pytest.ini (BUG-015 fix). That clear also strips any `--junit-xml=<path>` the user declared in addopts, so `pragma slice complete` and `pragma unlock` finish without writing `.pragma/pytest-junit.xml`. The PIL aggregator requires junit + spans to mark a permutation "verified"; with junit missing, every permutation reads "missing" on a fresh greenfield project - contradicting the docs. Fix: run_tests emits junit explicitly via `--junit-xml=<path>` so it survives the addopts clear, defaulting to `<cwd>/.pragma/pytest-junit.xml` which is where the aggregator looks.

permutations.description: run_tests emits a non-empty junit.xml at the default path when no explicit path is given
run_tests honours an explicit junit_xml parameter over the default
run_tests emits junit.xml even when a parent pytest.ini pins -q in addopts

requirements.title: report surfaces which artifact is missing instead of silent "missing"
requirements.description: BUG-020 companion. When the PIL aggregator computes "missing" for ≥50% of permutations because `.pragma/pytest-junit.xml` (or `.pragma/spans/`) is absent, `pragma report --human` and `--json` must include a diagnostic banner naming the absent artifact and the one-line fix. Without the banner, a user who hits the failure mode sees a silent wall of "missing" rows with "No remediation." - the worst possible UX for a tool whose whole point is trusted summaries.

permutations.description: pragma report --human shows a banner when junit.xml is missing
pragma report --human shows a banner when .pragma/spans/ is missing or empty
pragma report --human does not show a banner when both artifacts exist and the permutation count is correct

requirements.title: pre-release-smoke asserts end-to-end PIL reachability
requirements.description: `scripts/pre-release-smoke.sh` currently runs `pragma verify all` and checks that span files exist but never runs `pragma report` end-to-end. That blind spot is what let BUG-020 ship. Fix: after the first `slice complete` in the greenfield smoke section, assert `.pragma/pytest-junit.xml` exists and is non-empty, run `pragma report --human`, assert it contains at least one "ok" row and no banners, and run `pragma report --json` parsing for `summary.ok > 0` and `summary.missing == 0`. Exit non-zero if any assertion fails.

permutations.description: smoke script exits non-zero when junit.xml is not produced after slice complete
smoke script exits non-zero when pragma report --json shows any missing permutations

requirements.title: docs describe the loop as it actually is
requirements.description: The daily flow described in README / concepts.md / usage.md / reference.md / claude.md.tpl shows activate → red tests → unlock → implement → complete. After REQ-016 lands, that exact flow produces junit + spans automatically, and the PIL shows real coverage. Docs must state that - including what artifacts the PIL depends on - so a first-run user knows what to expect, and so the v1.0 done-criterion "PIL legible to 3 non-coders" becomes a question of legibility rather than reachability.

permutations.description: README Quick start explicitly names the `pragma report --human` step as part of the loop
docs/concepts.md states the PIL requires junit + spans and that pragma's own flows produce both
greenfield claude.md primer ends with a "make the PIL useful" section pointing at @trace + set_permutation
